title: digitalis canariensis f. glaberrima
intro: Digitalis canariensis f. glaberrima, also known as Canary Island Foxglove, is a variety of the Digitalis plant, which is a flowering plant in the foxglove family (Scrophulariaceae). It contains the medicinal compound digoxin, which has been used to treat various heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, by increasing the force of the heart's contractions and slowing its rate. The health benefits of Digitalis canariensis f. glaberrima include improved cardiovascular function, reduced risk of heart failure, and potentially reduced blood pressure. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and processing the leaves, which can be taken in capsule or tincture form to leverage its medicinal properties.
